meta.what: The GENERATIVE GRAMMAR of the Design DNA — rules and ratios, not hardcoded values. Every length, size, proportion, alignment and colour is DERIVED from a base + a ratio + the dials + the role/context. Nothing here is a fixed pixel that couldn't be re-derived.
meta.principle: A value has no inherent identity; it is defined by its relations. (Tim's relativistic-coherence principle, applied to design: a 90px margin is not 'ninety' — it is 'eleven base-units'; a surface is not '#FEFCF7' — it is 'high-lightness, near-zero-chroma, hue=warmth(dial)'.)
meta.provenance: ratios derived from the pixel-sampled / measured values in dna/tokens.json + the locked v2 mockups. Each carries Observed (literal measured) vs Inferred (ratio deduced) tags. grammar-2 folds in crystallized rules from dna/substrate-rule-harvest.md (Tim's prior thinking, AI-harvested from the substrate) — kept only the structural/relational rules that fit this look; aesthetics + engine-mechanics excluded.
meta.consumes: dna/tokens.json (the sampled anchors). Emits concrete values via dna/resolve.mjs.
meta.grammar3_note: grammar-3 adds the page-level rules harvested DIRECTLY from the deck (dna/DECK-HARVEST-2.md, 2026-06-10): shape semantics, movement line-verbs, ordinal colouring, foreign-hue quarantine, chrome modes, texture placement, content atoms, motion attachment + 4 invariants. Deck-level (multi-page) rules live in dna/sequence.json, not here.
meta.grammar4_note: grammar-4 (expansions-1): ordinal guards (ramp monotonicity is BUILD-ENFORCED in factory/build.mjs; max-n chunking), invariant 'checker' classification (predicate | model | build — 'model' marks the first lints that need an AI judge), still-stands-alone motion invariant, bullet speech-act lint.
meta.grammar5_note: grammar-5: THE STORY-TREE LAW. Every artifact is a tree; the tree is complete at every cut (depth-freedom) AND every fragment is self-contained (entry-freedom). Relations ADD, they never CARRY — chains, conjunctions, connectors and motion enrich a reading but nothing depends on them (the motion end-frame rule is this law's special case). Any (node, depth) selection is a valid artifact.
meta.grammar6_note: grammar-6: PROVISION (the abundance laws). Diagnosis 2026-06-10: pieces generated from the rules came out at '80%' — frames/voice/colour right, substance thin — because the rules encoded the deck's DISCIPLINE (restraints) but not its GENEROSITY (provision). These laws state what a page must PROVIDE. The organism layer (dna/organisms.json + dna/icons.json + surface/runtime/organisms.js) is their material.
meta.grammar10_note: grammar-10 (HARVEST-3): gold-density as register dial; quarantine's two strategies + two carve-outs; ordinal-axis-as-time; emphasis channels; two new invariants (one-starred-chip, gold-affirmation).

base._rule: Every length is unit × a step or a ratio. The reference_frame is the frame these were measured in — it is NOT a fixed canvas; proportions are fractions, so they hold at any frame size.

signature_ratio.name: the fifth
signature_ratio._rule: 1.5 recurs as the system's structural interval — it generates the radius steps AND the display-type steps. One interval, reused (universal composition).
signature_ratio._class: Inferred — see scales.radius and scales.type.display derivations
signature_ratio._tunable: The ratio is itself a knob, not a constant. 1.5 is the anchor (the measured look); register/density slide it within [1.25 dense/precise … 1.618 editorial/immersive]. Tighter ratio = denser hierarchy; wider = more dramatic scale contrast. (harvest: modular scale with one tunable ratio.)

commensurability._rule: Type and space are NOT two independent number sets — they share one root. A single base size (~16) sets body leading via line-height (×1.5 → ~24), and the spacing unit is that leading ÷ a small integer (24 ÷ 3 = 8). So the 8-grid and the type rhythm are commensurate multiples/divisors of one base, never magic numbers.
commensurability._class: Inferred (harvest crown-jewel: single-base commensurability) — reconciles base.unit=8 with the type scale under one source
commensurability._note: this is why the unit and the type ramp feel related rather than arbitrarily chosen.

space._class: Observed multiples-of-8 + Inferred step integers; emits match measured within ≤2px
space._note: spacing is never a raw number — it is a count of base-units. Steps trend {3,4,5,7,11} (an odd/near-prime climb), not linear.

vocabulary._what: THE EXTENSIBLE ADDRESS SPINE (rule-core R1b machinery, Tim unparked 2026-06-13). 'steps' above are the NAMED semantic subset the resolver exposes (resolve.mjs reads steps[name]); 'counts' here is the COMPLETE set of unit-counts that generate the --dna-space-{count} CSS addresses the chrome uses and lint-space polices. One rule (unit × count), one source: add a count here → build-surface.mjs emits its address → lint-space polices it. No other edit.

ingest_provision._what: WHERE the source ingest (or Tim) writes the canonical FINE words. Tim: 'the ingest needs the machinery to be written into.' The deck's true fine spacing — fractional counts the chrome currently drifts across — is ADMITTED by adding the count(s) here; addresses + lint follow from the single edit.
ingest_provision.status: AWAITING canonical words (source ingest, or Tim's blessing of the proposal). The spine holds only the in-use integer counts, so generation is pixel-identical until words are admitted.
ingest_provision.candidate_note: PROPOSAL, NOT admitted. 0.5→4px (half-unit), 0.75→6px, 1.5→12px (the signature-ratio interval; already radius-md), 2.5→20px. Admitting them lets the off-scale chrome literals (R1 ledger ≈635) be addressed and snapped. The naming + the fractional-address convention are the open decision — see docs/campaigns/rule-core. DO NOT auto-admit: the canonical words are the ingest's / Tim's to set.

vocabulary._class: machinery (R1b) — the extensible spine; 'steps' are its named aliases; the canonical fine words are ingest-derived

bonds._what: THE MISSING RULE made explicit (R1b end-to-end; Tim 2026-06-13: 'the proportions are DNA; the dials just adjust whatever the rules are, and the rules come from the DNA'). A gap's size is NOT a chosen number — it DERIVES from the STRENGTH of the bond between the two things it separates. This is grammar's own law (meta_principles.meaning_as_difference + space_allocation: 'the interval IS the relationship signal; space is proportional to the strength of the grouping boundary'), finally stated as a resolvable mapping: bond → a unit-count. The live engine (surface/runtime/surface.js DNA.space) resolves px = unit × count(bond) × density(dial) × tier. The engine holds NO values; load a different DNA and the same engine resolves ITS bonds. The 'sibling : section = 1:4' that earlier floated free is now HERE, as loadable data.
bonds.axis: bond STRENGTH is orthogonal to composition.relations (nesting/connection/override = relation TYPE — a nesting can be tight or loose). This ladder is the strength axis the space law names.

fused.is: within one atom — value+unit, icon+label; the tightest bond

kin.is: within a component — title→subtitle, a label and its field

grouped.is: siblings in a set — rows in a list, chips in a row

sectioned.is: group to group within a zone — plate to plate (= the 'compact' step)

zoned.is: zone to zone — the major divisions (= the 'standard' step)

framed.is: the outer margin — content to canvas edge (= the 'margin' step)

bonds._seed_note: SEEDED + FLAGGED for Tim — these counts are DATA, not code, and yours to tune. The ladder {0.5,1,2,3,5,11} is monotonic (tighter bond → smaller gap) and grounded: {3,5,11} REUSE existing step counts (compact/standard/margin — not a parallel set), {0.5,1,2} are the fine counts the chrome actually needs (unifies with vocabulary.ingest_provision.candidate_fine_counts). Retune any count → every gap that names that bond re-resolves live. Add/rename a bond → it's a new loadable word, no code change.
bonds._class: the relationship→count RULE (R1b end-to-end); values are DNA, loadable, Tim-tunable; resolved live by DNA.space

radius._class: Inferred ×1.5 geometric; matches measured within ≤1px — CLEAN
radius._note: the corner softness is a geometric progression, not arbitrary steps.
radius._addresses_note: name → exponent n (radius = unit × ratioⁿ). The single source for the --dna-radius-{name} addresses build-surface emits; extensible (add a name→n).
radius._harvested_note: the chrome's off-ramp radii (rollout sr-2, 'use whatever the hardcoded values are'). build-surface emits --dna-radius-{px}:{px}px for each — the harvested mirror, pixel-preserving; Phase-3 tuning reconciles these to the ratio scale (sm/md/lg/xl). 999 stays the exempt pill idiom.

display._class: Inferred ×1.5 from an 18 pivot; matches measured within ≤1px — CLEAN
display._addresses_note: name → exponent n (size = pivot × ratioⁿ). The single source for the --dna-type-{name} display addresses build-surface emits; extensible.

text.rule: reading-set — perceptual sizes clustered at the reading scale, NOT on the display ramp
text._class: Observed; deliberately off-ramp (reading comfort overrides the geometric scale below ~16px)

type._harvested_note: the chrome's off-ramp font-sizes (rollout sr-2, 'use whatever the hardcoded values are'), incl. sub-pixel sizes. build-surface emits --dna-type-{name}:{px}px where name = px with '.'→'_' (e.g. 9.5 → --dna-type-9_5). The harvested mirror, pixel-preserving; Phase-3 tuning reconciles these to the reading-set + display ramp.

proportion.rule: Zones are FRACTIONS of the cross-axis that sum to ~1 (the remainder is inter-zone gap). Because they are fractions, they hold at any frame size — this is the core 'nothing hardcoded' move for layout.

title-main-footer._note: single-line title. Two-line (superhead+title) title grows the title band to ~0.20 and shrinks main — the look wins over the tidy fraction.

split-weighted._note: the off-balance split — weights one side without obvious asymmetry

text_column.rule: a text column's width derives from the optimal reading measure (45–75 characters), NOT from an equal grid division — the measure sets the column, the grid follows

space_allocation.rule: whitespace is structural, not leftover (Ma): the space around an element is proportional to its importance and to the strength of its grouping boundary. Space replaces dividers; the interval IS the relationship signal. Outer margin is a first-class frame, sized from the same scale.

proportion._class: Observed measured from deck + v2 mockups
proportion._provenance: title 14 / main 72 / footer 10 also confirmed by the visual-factory template record

composition.rule: There are exactly THREE ways one element relates to another — every layout relation is one of these, never ad-hoc coupling.

relations.nesting: containment — a child sits inside a parent and inherits its context (the plate inside the zone, the figure inside the plate)
relations.connection: lateral — one element's output becomes another's context; this is the MOVEMENT layer (arrow / dashed transport / plug / hub between zones)
relations.override: contextual reframing — a scope changes resolution for everything beneath it without changing the elements themselves (a register or warmth shift over a subtree)

by_extreme_scale.rule: drive hierarchy with FEW elements at EXTREME scale ratios (one dominant ≈ 4–8× supporting), not many at moderate steps — one big figure + one small label reads instantly

dense_shifts_to_weight.rule: in dense/precise contexts, hierarchy shifts from SIZE contrast to WEIGHT contrast — adjacent levels differ by a small size step but a clear weight step
dense_shifts_to_weight._class: Observed (harvest); ties to the density dial

orthogonal_encoding.rule: no single property double-encodes a level — if size already sets it, weight/colour do not also. Priority: size > weight > colour/opacity. Each visual dimension carries one distinct meaning.

composition._class: Observed (harvest: three composition mechanisms; generalises my movement-connectors into the 'connection' relation)

resolution.rule: How any value is resolved when several scopes could define it: walk UP the scope chain and take the FIRST definition found — nearest scope wins. No specificity, no cascade ordering, no priority maths. The DNA root supplies defaults; anything closer overrides for everything beneath it. A generator never reads a fixed value — it resolves one by proximity.

context_is_a_scope.rule: responsiveness/context is NOT imperative branching (no isMobile flags) — the environment (frame size, register, modality) is itself a scope that supplies overrides; descendants re-resolve through the same nearest-wins walk

resolution._class: Observed (harvest: the single governing resolution rule; matches the visual-factory cascade global→project→user→runtime, first-found)
resolution._note: this is the operational core that makes 'nothing hardcoded' real — and it is exactly what the consuming factory already implements.

meta_principles._what: The roots beneath the rules — why the grammar takes the shape it does. (Tim's relativistic-coherence theory, recurring across the substrate.)

relativistic_identity.rule: Nothing has a fixed value; what anything IS emerges from where it sits, what flows through it, and what scope resolves around it. The generative root of the whole grammar — the formal statement of 'nothing hardcoded'.

meaning_as_difference.rule: A value's significance is the DELTA between it and its inherited context — influence scales with difference. An override equal to the parent has zero meaning. This is the single root beneath TWO of the colour relations: tonal zoning works BECAUSE the surface deltas are tiny (low influence → below attention), and gold punctuation works BECAUSE its delta from the restrained field is large (high influence). Express values as offsets from context; treat 'same as inherited' as a no-op.
meaning_as_difference._class: Observed (harvest) — unifies signal-economy + equal-lightness-zoning under one principle

identity_from_boundaries.rule: A thing is distinct because a boundary separates it from its surroundings; an element gets meaning from what contains it. Move it to a different container and what it IS changes, without its own properties changing. Position-in-hierarchy is a first-class determinant of appearance.

scale_invariance.rule: The same relative-measurement rule applies at every scale — between properties within an element, between elements, and between element and whole. A proportion rule defined once holds recursively from pixel to component to full layout.

dna_as_root.rule: The DNA constrains WITHOUT prescribing — it narrows the possibility space without collapsing it. Every concrete output is ONE valid collapse; the dials navigate the space. Specify the CLASS of acceptable forms (ratios, constraints, boundary conditions), never one fixed instance. This is the formal justification for 'it should widen' — the identity is a space, not a point.
dna_as_root._class: Observed (harvest) — the theory behind the dials and the widening directive

meta_principles.any_part_is_a_whole: any part is a whole — every node states its subtree completely at its own resolution; every fragment stands alone when extracted; reading depth is resolution; relations add, never carry
meta_principles.the_two_halves: every meaningful output is specific values in two halves resolving into one thing: the CALCULABLE half (addresses, counts, sizes, colours, registers — derivable by rule, 'no value typed in') and the AUTHORED half (the claims, the words, the cut, the route — the resolver's input, never its output). The boundary between them MOVES with the reader: what the reader already holds can be addressed, what they lack must be inlined. (Tim, 2026-06-11; see dna/SPACES.md)

_engine_layer_note: Some harvested rules are ENGINE mechanics, not identity grammar — the reactive cascade (topological regeneration, stale-hashing), registry unification (UI-units ≡ node-types), typed connectors, multi-dimensional placement. These belong to the consuming factory (vi-visual-dev), which already implements them, NOT to this DNA. Recorded here as a pointer so the boundary stays clean; see dna/substrate-rule-harvest.md for detail.

alignment.rule: Alignment is ROLE × LAYOUT-ARCHETYPE, never an absolute coordinate. The same element aligns differently depending on the composition it sits in.

title._note: centered for single-focus/stat/credible; left-edge-anchored in split/asymmetric layouts (var-12, var-16)

mark._note: the V mark is inset from the bottom-right corner by a margin-step, placed optically not mathematically

alignment._class: Inferred from the locked v2 mockups — this dimension was previously un-tokenised (the real gap)

color.rule: A colour is not a hex — it is a position on (lightness, chroma, hue) chosen by ROLE and AREA. Two relations govern everything:

equal_lightness_zoning.rule: surfaces share near-constant high lightness; they differ only by hue/chroma. Tonal zones read by hue-shift, NOT by darkness.
equal_lightness_zoning._class: Observed principle (pixel-sampled: warm/cool surfaces differ by hue at equal lightness)

signal_economy.rule: chroma is inversely proportional to area. Big area → ~0 chroma (a surface). Tiny area → high chroma (gold punctuation). Therefore gold can never be a fill — filling a large area with high chroma violates the relation.

accent_ramp.rule: the accent family is a SINGLE warm-metal hue; role selects a position on its (lightness↓, chroma) ramp.

ordinal_axis.rule: a COLLECTION steps the warm-metal axis in order — position in the sequence = position on the axis (gold→ochre→bronze→charcoal); brightness maps to attention/proximity
ordinal_axis.evidence: deck s10/s13 pill rows; s7 role-circles; s2 mesh fading gold-centre→bronze-periphery

guards.monotonic_lightness: the ramp must darken monotonically — luminance order is what makes a series READ as a series; enforced at build time (factory/build.mjs throws)
guards.max_n: ordinal colouring holds to ~7 items; beyond that, CHUNK into groups first (the grid archetype exists for this)

foreign_hue_quarantine.rule: non-axis hues may appear ONLY inside partner-evidence slots (logo-wall, person logos) — foreign colour enters as evidence, never as design
foreign_hue_quarantine.evidence: deck s11/s13 (NVIDIA green, Google colours, Oracle red); semantic.success green only on money-flow arrows (screenshot)

chrome_modes.rule: two chrome modes keyed to the ground: on IMAGERY — dark-glass controls + frosted white cards; on PAPER — outlined light controls. This is also the document-surface vs product-surface line.
chrome_modes.evidence: deck s1/s16 (charcoal MENU pill, frosted info card) vs s2–s15 (outlined pills)

warmth_dial.rule: warmth ∈ [0,100] interpolates surfaces in OKLCH between cool and warm anchors at constant lightness (perceptual lerp, never sRGB).
warmth_dial._class: Observed anchors; OKLCH interpolation = Inferred best practice (confirmed wanted by the visual-factory)

hatch.rule: −45° drafted hairline, pitch = unit ± 1 (8–10px), opacity by ground: 0.25–0.35 over warm, 0.40–0.50 over near-white
hatch._class: Observed; pitch ties to the base unit

texture._principle: texture is a thin drafted signature, corner-zoned, never tiled over content

dials._targets_note: RC-C (2026-06-14): each dial DECLARES what it modifies, by ROLE/family — not a hand-list. The engine reads targets.modifies (DNA.dialsFor) and applies the dial; it names no dial. A new token of a targeted family falls under the dial with no edit; a new dial enters by declaring its target. Dial POSITION is a knob; the transform (by) is the mechanism.

targets.by: interpolate cool↔warm anchors at constant lightness

density.rule: density multiplies the space scale — rich tightens WARM elements (more info, never noise); spacious loosens.

targets._consumed_by: DNA.dialsFor → space / injectSpace (reads this, no hardcoded dial name)

targets.by: invert lightness (signed axis on luminosity; magnitude preserved — |ΔL| unchanged, sign flipped)

polarity.provenance: Tim, Packet 01 Block I (un-deferred 2026-06-14: 'A and b'): 'inverting a scalar on an axis, direction being one'. PREDICTED-FROM-PRINCIPLE — repo-grep found no built artifact (the connectors' gather(in/out) is the law arriving early); Tim's words are the provenance.
polarity._slice: LUMINOSITY only (dark surfaces derive from light by lightness inversion, DNA.invertL). The HEART — depth inverting (shadow→glow, out→in) + the signed-axis generalization (what else carries hidden sign) — is deeper theory to develop WITH Tim (Packet 01 Block I). Held as a DIAL, decoupled from depth (Tim: 'this isn't a mode, it's a dial'); candidate 5th axis + hidden 6th (sign vs magnitude) — unconverged.

scale_tiers.rule: Each tier re-bases the unit; ratios and proportions are unchanged, so the identity holds from poster to component. base_unit(tier) = unit × tier_factor.
scale_tiers._class: Inferred — multi-scale by unit re-basing; proportions are scale-free by construction

invariants.predicate: hue ∈ warm-metal family OR chroma≈0 (surface/ink) OR small-semantic-mark
invariants.message: accent only on the warm-metal axis

invariants.predicate: gold_area_fraction < 0.02
invariants.message: gold is punctuation, not a fill (signal economy)

invariants.predicate: max_lightness_delta_between_surfaces < 0.03
invariants.message: tonal zoning by hue at equal lightness, not by darkness

invariants.predicate: color == accent.charcoal
invariants.message: titles are charcoal, never pure black

invariants.predicate: !title.text.endsWith('.')
invariants.message: titles never end in a period

invariants.predicate: count(high-chroma-or-largest-figure) == 1
invariants.message: one clear focal point, generous space

invariants.predicate: value % base_unit ∈ {0, ±2}
invariants.message: spacing derives from the base unit, not arbitrary px

invariants.predicate: page bottom edge carries the gold diagonal hatch ground line (paper AND imagery)
invariants.message: every page closes with the hatch baseline

invariants.predicate: elements overlapping a zone boundary ∈ movement layer (connector|badge|plug|arrow)
invariants.message: only movement may cross a seam

invariants.applies_to: any colour outside the warm-metal axis & semantic marks
invariants.predicate: parent slot ∈ {logo-wall, partner-evidence}
invariants.message: foreign colour enters as evidence, never as design

invariants.predicate: count(octagon) ≤ 1 per page; facet-degree inversely proportional to frequency
invariants.message: shape rarity mirrors gold rarity

invariants.predicate: OKLCH lightness strictly decreasing across stops
invariants.message: the ordinal ramp must darken monotonically

invariants.predicate: n ≤ 7, else chunked into groups
invariants.message: ordinal colour blurs past ~7 — chunk first

invariants.predicate: the final frame satisfies all page invariants without the motion
invariants.message: print/PDF collapse motion — the end frame must stand alone

invariants.predicate: glyph matches statement kind: triangle=claim, checkbox=completed fact
invariants.message: a claim may not wear a checkbox

invariants.predicate: the cut reads as a complete telling at its own resolution
invariants.message: every cut is complete — skimming is reading, not skipping

invariants.applies_to: any bounded unit taken as root, any rank beneath it
invariants.predicate: the same-rank items, read in written order, form a complete telling of the root at their grain
invariants.message: every row is a story, under every root — deck titles chain, page zone-headings chain, zone labels chain
invariants.status: derived-from-principle (2026-06-11, Tim) — generalizes title_chain and cut-complete strata to every root; verified only at deck root / title rank so far. See sequence.reading_axes.

invariants.applies_to: every brief / artifact record (generation_ladder seed stage)
invariants.predicate: the record carries subject_region + reader + occasion + purpose (dna/reader.json brief_schema)
invariants.message: no purpose recorded, no judgment possible — 'good' is fit to a purpose, never absolute
invariants.status: derived-from-principle (2026-06-11, Tim: "'good' as an outcome is only relative to the specific purpose that specific thing was for")

invariants.applies_to: any change between two views (movement, transition, or state — motion.json registers_of_change)
invariants.predicate: the change is animated as its register with shared anchors carrying orientation; a hard cut with no shared anchor is a violation unless it is a deliberate bookend
invariants.message: nothing teleports — the canvas is a place and changes are journeys on it
invariants.status: tours-direct + Tim Geldard's direction (2026-06-11): 'meant to feel like movement, not teleporting — not a reload'

invariants.predicate: the landing view shares enough with the origin to re-orient AND differs enough to progress — neither a wasted hop nor a disorienting one
invariants.message: budget the change: enough sameness to know where you are, enough difference to have gone somewhere
invariants.status: tours-direct (Tim Geldard's virtual tours: point density follows rate-of-change of the view, not distance)

invariants.predicate: no contradiction across siblings — a figure stated in one equals the same figure in all; a coined term means one thing everywhere; claims agree
invariants.message: siblings must not contradict — a contradiction between the company deck and the product deck is a credibility wound
invariants.status: derived-from-principle (2026-06-11) — the first CROSS-ARTIFACT invariant; testable the moment two siblings exist in the system

invariants.applies_to: any extracted node + its subtree (a slide, a plate, a couplet)
invariants.predicate: the fragment is a complete self-contained story without its siblings or ancestors
invariants.message: any part is a whole — fragments travel

invariants.applies_to: all chain devices (title conjunctions, connectors, motion, motif recurrence)
invariants.predicate: removing the chain device loses momentum but never sense
invariants.message: relations add, never carry

invariants.predicate: ≥1 tonal surface (zone or band) reaches the canvas edge
invariants.message: compose with zones, not floating cards

invariants.predicate: carries ambient + primary + detail layers
invariants.message: a diagram is a world, not a legend

invariants.predicate: count(distinct figures) ≥ 4
invariants.message: evidence arrives dense — one star, several supporting

invariants.predicate: ≥1 inset level inside the plate (statrow, nested box, chip)
invariants.message: exercise the recursion — plates contain

invariants.predicate: at most one chip in a row carries the gold star (the focal value)
invariants.message: one star per row — signal economy on chips

invariants.predicate: affirmation checks are GOLD (brand), negation crosses muted red; status-success (sage) is reserved for live state, never for comparative affirmation
invariants.message: in comparisons the brand affirms in gold — sage is for status, not victory

shape.rule: shape carries entity TYPE; the more faceted the shape, the rarer it must be (signal economy in geometry)

octagon.rarity: at most once per page — usually once per DECK
octagon.evidence: deck s4 (the V hub, the deck's only octagon)

circle.evidence: deck s2 mesh, s4 ring, s7 roles, s13 portraits

movement.rule: dashed = in motion; solid hairline = structure. Dashed lines conjugate into three verbs.

transport.evidence: deck s5 (the plug between the two panels)

gather.form: dashed brackets converging to a single point
gather.evidence: deck s6 (under the grids → centre icon)

descend.form: dashed drop-lines from a row down to a plug/icon

arrowheads.rule: two-tone — bronze shaft, gold head: direction emphasised only at the terminal

movement.seam_law: only movement-layer elements (connectors, badges, plugs) may sit ON or cross a zone boundary — nothing else breaks the frame
movement.evidence_seam: deck s6/s9 — engine badges overlap both columns; var-13's -30px engine overlap

texture_placement.blueprint: underlay appears only NEAR TITLES (top corners), never under content — texture zones are role-bound
texture_placement.hatch: the gold diagonal hatch is the page's closing ground line — every page, including over imagery
texture_placement.evidence: deck s2–s15 corners; hatch on all 16 slides incl. the renders

stat_couplet.form: gold figure LEFT, grey label RIGHT; units ride in the gold ('$430B+', '9.4 hrs/wk', '91x')

bullet_semantics.rule: the glyph declares the statement's kind

person_row.form: round photo + gold name + grey role caption

coined_term.form: quotes + italic emphasis on first major use

raised_chip.form: embossed white box holding a key figure, soft bottom shadow, side label

motion.rule: motion attaches ONLY to movement-layer elements and media slots (diagram/image) — chrome never moves; the things that move are the things that MEAN movement
motion.tokens: durations in tokens.json motion_ms (path_draw 480ms anticipates dashed-line drawing)
motion.evidence: deck GIF placeholders sit exactly on diagram/media slots: s4, s5, s7 + screenshot

provision.rule: restraint shapes the page; PROVISION fills it — both are the identity. A page that honours every restraint but provides nothing reads as 80% of the source.

zones_not_plates.rule: compose by DIVIDING THE CANVAS INTO SURFACES — full-bleed tonal zones and edge-to-edge bands — not by floating cards on a void; plates live INSIDE zones
zones_not_plates.default: argue/show lead with a full-bleed zone; prove leads with bands; precise may stay open paper
zones_not_plates.evidence: deck s2 (left 45% tinted zone, edge to edge), s5 (two large panels ~75% of canvas), s12/s16 (full-height zones) vs gen3-v1's floating cards

diagram_atmosphere.rule: a diagram is a WORLD with three layers — ambient (the weather: a fading mesh, faint texture), primary (the argument: nodes + movement), detail (the grounding: labels, small strips) — a primary layer alone is a legend, not a world
diagram_atmosphere.evidence: deck s2: person-mesh (ambient) + 5-circle core w/ crossing arrows (primary) + ~40 file-format strip (detail)

evidence_density.rule: evidence arrives DENSE: a prove page carries ≥4 distinct figures (one star + supporting); an argue page names its stakes in numbers
evidence_density.evidence: deck s9 carries eight figures; s2's consequences box carries four

nesting_depth.rule: exercise the recursion: page → zone → plate → INSET → chip; credible/prove plates carry at least one inset level (statrow boxes, nested panels)
nesting_depth.evidence: deck s9 embossed boxes inside plates; s2 consequences panel inside the right zone; grammar's plate-internal was already encoded but unexercised

controlled_density.rule: the controlled-density device: MANY small quiet entries compressed into ONE calm box — density raised, noise not (the tension register's tool, but available everywhere)
controlled_density.evidence: deck s2 file-extension strip (~40 entries), s2 consequences box, s3 stat trio

fill.rule: zones get FILLED — a void is not calm, it is absence; G3 ('kill the void') applies at the content scale, not just the page scale
fill.evidence: the v2 audit of generation 1–2 killed page voids; gen3-v1 reintroduced them inside plates and rows

iconography.rule: fine-line icons are a TEXTURE CHANNEL, not decoration — strips on rails, marks in plates, anchors in diagrams; a page with zero icons has a silent channel
iconography.evidence: the 9-icon strip appears on four deck slides; small icons live in plates and boxes throughout

rules_corpus.gold_density_register: gold density RIDES THE REGISTER dial: marketing collateral runs gold-rich (gold section headlines, gold actives everywhere); investor decks run austere (gold = figures + focal only). Not a broken rarity law — a dial. [product corpus vs deck corpus]
rules_corpus.quarantine_strategies: TWO ways to handle foreign marks: (1) true hues confined to logo/partner strips; (2) RE-SKIN functional foreign marks into the axis (bronze social icons, company-info p12). Plus two carve-outs: evidence screenshots keep their hues (content, not design — 17p p3 collage); role_hues are a sanctioned product family (tokens.color.role_hues).
rules_corpus.ordinal_axis_as_time: the metal axis encodes TEMPORAL DISTANCE: gold = now/near, bronze = later/far (15p p13 gold 3–18mo vs bronze 18–48mo frames; capital-raise p8 ramp-gradient time axes; charts use the ramp: pie p15 pitch-deck).
rules_corpus.emphasis_channels: four inline emphasis channels, in descending frequency: bold (key terms), italic (coined/captions/asides), gold (figures inline in sentences — numbers-are-sentences), underline (RARE — once in the corpus: 'Australia', pitch-deck p12).
